stimulus.content: In a market economy, businesses and individuals make most economic decisions about what to produce and sell. In a command economy, the government controls most production and sets prices. Many countries today have a mix of both systems.

stem: What is the main difference between a market economy and a command economy?

choices.text: Who decides what goods are produced and sold
Whether the country has any natural resources
How large the population of the country is
What language the people of the country speak

answer_explanation: In a market economy, individuals and businesses make economic decisions. In a command economy, the government controls production and prices. The key difference is who makes economic decisions.
